ogTitle: West Swan vs West Toodyay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Swan and West Toodyay.
heading: West Swan vs West Toodyay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Swan, Western Australia and West Toodyay, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Swan has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to West Toodyay (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Swan or West Toodyay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Swan has a flood risk score of 54/100, while West Toodyay scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Swan scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. West Toodyay scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Swan or West Toodyay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Swan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
